title: rootless podman units should wait for podman-user-wait-network-online.service
body:
### Issue Description

In #24305 I made quadlet units wait for podman-user-wait-network-online.service, however the same network startup issue still exists for our static podman units that start containers such as podman-restart.service and podman-kube@.service.
They should use the same After=podman-user-wait-network-online.service in the rootless case to make them work the same.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Have lingering enabled and try to start a container via podman-restart.service on boot before the network is ready, see https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/22197



### Describe the results you received

pasta failed to start as the network was not yet ready

### Describe the results you expected

unit should start after the network is ready

### podman info output

```yaml
podman 5.3.0
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Needs a slow network setup to reproduce (I was able to trigger this by reassigning new mac address to libvirt VMs then on the next boot network setup took several seconds which was long enough to start the user unit to early when lingering is enabled)

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Any update on this? I have the issue issue:

```bash
$ systemctl --user --failed
  UNIT                                    LOAD   ACTIVE SUB    DESCRIPTION                                         
● podman-user-wait-network-online.service loaded failed failed Wait for system level network-online.target as user.
```

From the user perspective, quick story:

```
Podman Version:       5.4.2
OS: 'debian 13 trixie' and 'KUbuntu 25.04'
```

I recently gave a shot to Quadlets. I have a simple script with
```
systemctl --user daemon-reload
(for each service) systemctl --user restart my.service
```
to restart network and 4 containers.

I was scratching my head for 2 evenings why Quadlets are so HORRIBLY slow and I started digging into it. Script took 7,5 minutes to restart services.
```
$ time ~/Documents/deploy-quadlets.sh 
real	7m36.605s
user	0m0.026s
sys	0m0.007s
```
Luckily, I found this issue and several linked to it. It turned out that 90s timeout x 5 quickly adds up. With proper workaround it works as I would expect.

> Luckily, I found this issue and several linked to it. It turned out that 90s timeout x 5 quickly adds up. With proper workaround it works as I would expect.

What work around did you use?

This is how I solved it for me:

```bash
# Create dummy unit to make podman-user-wait-network-online.service work
cat <<-'EOF' | tee --append /etc/systemd/system/dummy-network-online.service
[Unit]
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
Description=Dummy unit to make podman-user-wait-network-online.service work

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=echo "This unit wants network-online.target"

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable dummy-network-online.service
```

Edit: 
Credits: Found the original fix: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24796#issuecomment-2527822425

> What work around did you use?

Personally, I've disabled default dependencies for network and all containers. It works well for me since then.
```
[Quadlet]
DefaultDependencies=false
```
